survivors.semantic_target: period-end shares used instead of weighted average
survivors.root_cause_detail: Runner reads shares_outstanding raw value per year without binding each numeric value to a share_basis enum (WEIGHTED_AVERAGE_BASIC | WEIGHTED_AVERAGE_DILUTED | PERIOD_END_BASIC | PERIOD_END_DILUTED | SPLIT_ADJUSTED_HISTORICAL). Verifier recomputes EPS numeric only; cannot detect period-end masquerading as weighted average because the basis tag is not propagated as a first-class binding.
survivors.downstream_risk: EPS overstated when period-end shares < weighted average (typical for issuers). Valuation engine receives inflated EPS → P/E too low → wrong investment signal.
survivors.remediation_design: 1) Extend MetricInput model with mandatory share_basis binding (numeric_value, share_basis, source_metric_id). 2) Structural validator (new module) checks that EPS uses WEIGHTED_AVERAGE_* and emits SHARE_BASIS_MISMATCH when period-end is bound. 3) Verifier independently reads the binding, not just the numeric value.

survivors.root_cause_detail: Same as MUT-FUND-008. No EPS_BASIS_MISMATCH detector because basis is not bound per metric.
survivors.downstream_risk: Diluted shares > basic → EPS understated → conservative bias but inconsistent with declared BASIC export. Breaks cross-period comparability and parent IR EPS basis contract.
survivors.remediation_design: Reuse the share_basis binding above; structural validator emits EPS_BASIS_MISMATCH when EPS_BASIC metric has shares bound with basis DILUTED (and vice versa).

survivors.changed_fields: ROE denominator metric (avg -> ending) without warning
survivors.root_cause_detail: Runner computes avg_equity but has no binding recording whether the formula used AVERAGE or ENDING. When mutation feeds ending equity as avg (same numeric shape), verifier cannot tell whether the value is the canonical average or a silently-substituted ending balance.
survivors.downstream_risk: Ending equity typically > average equity (growing book) → ROE understated; (shrinking book) → overstated. Investor sees distorted profitability.
survivors.remediation_design: 1) Bind each balance-sheet metric to denominator_basis (AVERAGE_COMMON_EQUITY | ENDING_COMMON_EQUITY | AVERAGE_TOTAL_ASSETS | ENDING_TOTAL_ASSETS). 2) Ending-balance fallback only legal with rule_id + warning + quality downgrade + calculation trace. 3) Verifier independently checks the binding is AVERAGE for ROE/ROA/EM or that fallback rule is satisfied.

survivors.root_cause_detail: Verifier recomputes ROA = npat/denominator and compares numbers. If mutation substitutes equity (which may numerically equal the asset input in degenerate fixtures) the verifier sees the same number and PASSes. There is no metric_id identity binding on formula inputs.
survivors.downstream_risk: ROA misreported as ROE. Misleading capital-efficiency signal.
survivors.remediation_design: Each formula call binds (numerator_metric_id, denominator_metric_id) into the calculation trace. Verifier independently reads the trace and confirms ROA's denominator metric_id is TOTAL_ASSETS (or AVERAGE_TOTAL_ASSETS), not EQUITY.

survivors.semantic_target: DuPont EM uses ending instead of average
survivors.root_cause_detail: EM = avg_assets / avg_equity. Mutation feeds ending balances; since both numerator and denominator shift consistently the numeric ratio may still satisfy the |reconstructed - direct| < 0.005 check, masking the basis swap. No basis binding on DuPont component inputs.
survivors.downstream_risk: DuPont decomposition distorts leverage signal. EM inflated for growing companies.
survivors.remediation_design: DuPont components inherit denominator_basis binding from their inputs. Verifier checks AT, EM, ROE-check all use the same basis family (avg with avg, ending only under registered fallback).

survivors.root_cause_detail: Status is a free-form string on MetricResult. No invariant enforcement that INPUT_INCOMPLETE cannot be upgraded to VALID without the underlying input becoming complete. Downstream export gate only checks status in (VALID, VALID_NEGATIVE) so a swapped status leaks straight through.
survivors.downstream_risk: Engine reports VALID on incomplete data → valuation consumes garbage EPS/BVPS → wrong target price. Critical for parent IR integrity.
survivors.remediation_design: 1) Applicability decision recorded as immutable per-metric status_decision with required_inputs list. 2) Status invariant: VALID requires all required_inputs non-None and not MISSING. 3) Export gate additionally requires status_decision present (not just status string). 4) Verifier independently re-derives applicability from input completeness and compares.

survivors.changed_fields: income period TTM, denominator period Q4 (quarter)
survivors.root_cause_detail: Runner collapses every metric to latest-year value with no period tag. Period identity (FY | TTM | Q1..Q4 | H1 | H2) is never bound to the value, so cross-period mixing inside one ratio cannot be detected.
survivors.downstream_risk: TTM numerator / quarterly denominator inflates ratio ~4x. ROE/ROA/NPM all distorted.
survivors.remediation_design: Each MetricInput binds a period tag (fiscal_year + period_kind). Ratio formulas require numerator and denominator period_kind compatible (FY~FY, TTM~TTM, or Q~Q with same quarter) before computing.

survivors.semantic_target: average denominator replaced by ending without warning
survivors.changed_fields: denominator basis avg -> ending, no warning
survivors.root_cause_detail: Twin of MUT-FUND-013 but generalised to any average-based denominator. Same missing binding.
survivors.downstream_risk: Same as MUT-FUND-013: ratio distortion when book grows/shrinks intra-period.
survivors.remediation_design: Covered by the denominator_basis_policy introduced for MUT-FUND-013. Regression test uses a non-ROE ratio (ROA or EM) to confirm generality.

survivors.root_cause_detail: Phase 4 has empty implementation/peers/ directory. Runner never constructs a peer set, never enforces minimum coverage (>=3 per VFA-REQ-026), never blocks ranking on insufficient coverage.
survivors.downstream_risk: Cherry-picked peer set biases benchmark. Investor sees artificially favourable ranking.
survivors.remediation_design: Implement peer_engine.py with: peer_set_validation (target excluded from own peer set), minimum_peer_coverage (>=3 with data), benchmark_calculation per registered policy, ranking_eligibility gate, provenance_binding per peer source. Result emitted as PeerResult material bound to downstream export.

survivors.root_cause_detail: No policy registry; no central-tendency computation; impossible to detect a policy swap.
survivors.downstream_risk: Mean sensitive to outliers → skewed benchmark → wrong relative-value signal.
survivors.remediation_design: Peer engine emits benchmark with declared policy_id (MEDIAN | MEAN | TRIMMED_MEAN). Verifier recomputes both median and mean from the peer set and confirms the reported value matches the declared policy; mismatch -> PEER_POLICY_VIOLATION.

survivors.root_cause_detail: Without a peer engine there is no period alignment check between target and peers.
survivors.downstream_risk: TTM target ranked against quarterly peers → incomparable ratios.
survivors.remediation_design: Peer engine requires every peer metric share target's period_kind (or registered normalization). Mismatch -> drop peer; if coverage drops below minimum -> PEER_COVERAGE_INSUFFICIENT.

survivors.fixture: peer-comparison fixtures (peer set with <3 valid peers)
survivors.root_cause_detail: No coverage gate -> no way to block ranking.
survivors.downstream_risk: Ranking on 1-2 peers is statistically meaningless.
survivors.remediation_design: ranking_eligibility flag set iff coverage >= 3 AND outlier policy applied. Downstream export must drop ranking block when ineligible.

survivors.changed_fields: reporting_scope of income or equity CONSOLIDATED -> STANDALONE
survivors.root_cause_detail: Runner has no notion of reporting_scope / consolidation_scope / attribution_scope / denominator_scope. Each metric is a value; nothing records whether it is consolidated or standalone. Cross-scope mixing inside one ratio is undetectable.
survivors.downstream_risk: Consolidated revenue / standalone equity → inflated ROE/ROA. Attributable-to-parent NI / total-group equity → wrong EPS. Critical for groups with minority interests.
survivors.remediation_design: Each MetricInput binds reporting_scope (CONSOLIDATED | STANDALONE) and attribution_scope (ATTRIBUTABLE_TO_PARENT | TOTAL_GROUP). Ratio formulas require matching scope across numerator and denominator. Verifier independently checks the binding chain from output back to normalized input.

survivors.changed_fields: numerator attribution ATTRIBUTABLE, denominator attribution TOTAL
survivors.root_cause_detail: Twin of MUT-FUND-024 specifically for attribution_scope. No binding -> undetectable.
survivors.downstream_risk: ROE / BVPS understated for groups with large minority (e.g. BVH). EPS basis breaks.
survivors.remediation_design: Covered by scope_policy above. Regression test focuses on attribution mismatch where consolidation_scope is identical (so the only signal is attribution_scope).

survivors.changed_fields: source_id / source_date / source_type removed from EPS material
survivors.root_cause_detail: Phase 4 evidence_manifest only hashes request/output at the top level. No per-metric provenance record (source_id, source_date, source_type, collector_metric_id). Verifier has nothing to check against.
survivors.downstream_risk: Untraceable numbers in parent report. Audit chain broken; cannot defend a target price in front of compliance.
survivors.remediation_design: Each material MetricResult carries a provenance record resolving metric_result -> formula_inputs -> normalized_inputs -> collector_metric_id -> source_id -> raw_evidence. Each link hash-bound. Verifier independently checks presence + hash freshness. Stale hash -> STALE_EVIDENCE_BINDING.

oracle_defect_check.policy: No survivor is auto-classified as INVALID_MUTATION_ORACLE. Each mutation target exists in the registry; each fixture precondition is constructible; each mutation produces a material semantic change (different basis / different period / different scope / removed provenance). Oracle integrity holds for all 15.
